class_features.name: Pact Magic
class_features.summary: Warlocks cast prepared Warlock spells using Pact Magic slots. Pact Magic slots are all the same level and return on a Short or Long Rest.

class_features.name: Eldritch Invocations
class_features.summary: Warlocks gain invocation options that modify spells, grant at-will magic, or define pact benefits. Invocation counts are recorded in class progression.

class_features.name: Magical Cunning
class_features.summary: After a 1-minute rite, the Warlock regains expended Pact Magic slots up to half their maximum, rounded up, once per Long Rest.

class_features.name: Spellcasting
class_features.summary: The Wizard prepares spells from a spellbook, uses Intelligence as spellcasting ability, and starts with six level 1 Wizard spells in the spellbook.

class_features.name: Arcane Recovery
class_features.summary: After finishing a Short Rest, the Wizard can recover expended spell slots with combined levels up to half Wizard level, rounded up.

class_features.name: Bardic Inspiration
class_features.summary: The Bard grants a Bardic Inspiration die to another creature, which can add it to a D20 Test after seeing the roll.

class_features.name: Rage
class_features.summary: The Barbarian enters a combat fury that grants resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage and improves Strength-based attacks.

class_features.name: Sneak Attack
class_features.summary: Once per turn, the Rogue deals extra damage when they hit with a qualifying attack and satisfy the feature conditions.

class_features.name: Channel Divinity
class_features.summary: The Cleric channels divine power into options such as Divine Spark and Turn Undead.

class_features.name: Wild Shape
class_features.summary: The Druid magically assumes a Beast form known to them, following the Wild Shape restrictions and uses.

class_features.name: Action Surge
class_features.summary: The Fighter can take one additional action on their turn, excluding the Magic action.

class_features.name: Monk's Focus
class_features.summary: The Monk spends Focus Points to fuel features such as Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind.

class_features.name: Lay on Hands
class_features.summary: The Paladin uses a healing pool to restore Hit Points or remove certain conditions from a touched creature.

class_features.name: Favored Enemy
class_features.summary: The Ranger gains Hunter's Mark preparation and special use support from their class progression.

class_features.name: Innate Sorcery
class_features.summary: The Sorcerer taps innate magic to improve Sorcerer spellcasting for a limited duration.
